body.title: Code index: schema + Nanny Ogg sweep for .py files
body.result: clan.code_index table (m069/m070), code_sweep.py AST walker, nightly_code_sweep in Nanny default schedule — 4,999 symbols on first run, 14 tests
body.intention: I intend that every Python function and class in the repo is indexed with its signature, docstring, and a file pointer so semantic search resolves code symbols without requiring full-file reads.
body.description:
Create a code_index table (path, symbol, kind, summary, embedding, content_hash, updated_at). Wire a Nanny Ogg cron job that sweeps devices/**/*.py and unseen_university/**/*.py using AST parsing: extracts function/class name + docstring + signature as the summary, hashes the body for staleness detection. On hash change: update summary, re-queue for embedding via T-auto-embed-on-creation's queue. Summary stored in code_index; embedding stored via embedding_queue drain.

**Affected files:** DB migration (new table: code_index), devices/nanny_ogg/sweeps/code_sweep.py (new), devices/nanny_ogg/cron_backend.py (register sweep job)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; AST-level parsing (not full-file text) for summaries; content_hash for staleness
**Scope boundary:** .py files only (not .md, not chat logs — those are separate). Function + class level granularity. Out of scope: semantic routing improvements, .md file indexing.
**Completion criteria:** code_index table exists in DB; cron sweep job registered in Nanny Ogg; after running sweep manually, >= 50 rows in code_index with non-null summary; at least one row has a matching embedding_queue entry (or completed embedding).
